top_signals.title: Trump Arrives in Turkey With Iran War at Top of Agenda
top_signals.summary: President Donald Trump lands in Ankara, Turkey for the NATO summit where he will start the day visiting with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan. Bloomberg's Annmarie Hordern is on the ground with the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Chinese lidar maker with Nvidia ties accused of being cyber risk for U.S.
top_signals.summary: Hesai Technology was blacklisted as a national security threat in 2024 by the U.S. Department of Defense, which designated Hesai as a Chinese military entity.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: The U.S. Army Just Took a Historic Step to Break China's Rare Earth Dominance
top_signals.summary: The U.S. Army has placed REalloys at the center of America’s drive to rebuild its heavy rare earth supply chain, selecting the company to build and operate the first-ever commercial critical mineral processing operation on a U.S. military installation. REalloys (NASDAQ: ALOY) plans to build a heavy rare earth processing complex at the Tooele Army Depot in Utah capable of refining dysprosium and terbium, two of the most strategically important rare earth elements used in high-temperature permanent magnets for defense systems. For the first…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Saudi-Flagged Crude Oil Tanker Sustained Damage in Hormuz Strait
top_signals.summary: A crude oil tanker sailing under the flag of Saudi Arabia sustained damage while navigating the Strait of Hormuz, a person with knowledge of the matter said, the latest incident to highlight that navigation through the waterway remains risky.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Le Pen Judgement Day, Samsung Drops after Profit Beat | The Opening Trade 7/7/2026
top_signals.summary:
The outcome of Marine Le Pen’s appeal of her conviction for embezzling public funds could shape French and European politics for years. The decision due today from the three-judge appellate panel will determine not only whether the 57-year-old three-time presidential candidate gets another shot at the Élysée, but whether Jordan Bardella, her 30-year-old protégé, becomes the National Rally’s face in what polls suggest could be the far-right party’s best opportunity yet to win power.

The chip sector came under more pressure, with Samsung Electronics, whose shares have more than doubled this year, plunging as much as 10% despite reporting a 19-fold jump in profit. 

The Opening Trade has everything you need to know as markets open across Europe. With analysis you won't find anywhere else, we break down the biggest stories of the day and speak to top guests who have skin in the game. Hosted by Anna Edwards and Guy Johnson. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Germany set to become first international site for ATACMS missile production
top_signals.summary: The move addresses a bottleneck, as Lockheed Martin has been winding down ATACMS output at its Camden, Arkansas, site while prioritizing newer missiles.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Trader loses $2M in ‘same-block backrun extraction’ exploit
top_signals.summary: One crypto trader noted the $2 million loss could have been prevented had the victim read the transaction route before signing the transaction.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Porsche 911 GT3 S/C review: Is new 194 mph convertible is car of the millennium?
top_signals.summary: Motoring journalists have probably lavished more praise on the Porsche 911 GT3 than any other new car this millennium. It has slain supercars, won comparison tests and consistently been hailed as a benchmark for driving dynamics. Over 28 years and eight generations, it has rarely put a semi-slick tyre wrong. Every new GT3 thus far [...]
